title: Import a representative legacy C-LARA project corpus and add batch import tooling
notes: Suggestion #9 from admin export (submitted by mannyrayner on 2026-05-09), updated by follow-up suggestion #2 on 2026-05-10. Now that supported legacy C-LARA JSON ZIP import is available, import a substantial representative corpus of legacy projects into C-LARA-2. The Adelaide legacy export folder currently contains per-project bundle directories named by project number, which makes browsing hard, but each directory has a top-level metadata.json containing fields such as id, title, l2, l1, owner_username, size_bytes, and sha256. First add a script that scans a folder of such bundle directories and creates a global metadata file aggregating this per-bundle metadata; this can be run once for the Adelaide download. Then refactor the existing Import source ZIP affordance into a dedicated Import from ZIP view with two modes: (1) the current simple local ZIP upload/import path; (2) an admin-only legacy-server corpus mode that reads the global metadata file, lets the admin search/select bundles by partial title, owner/userid, source language, and target language, and then imports the selected bundle through the same importer. Design this admin corpus mode so it can later support selecting and importing many bundles in one run, with heartbeat-style progress updates and per-bundle success/failure diagnostics. Start manually with roughly a dozen legacy ZIPs to exercise the importer against real data, collect diagnostics, and identify unsupported legacy fields or media/reference edge cases. Use the imported corpus as test material for ISSUE-0001 hosted compiled legacy content registration.
